text: Running economy is a better predictor of distance running performance than VO2max in elite runners who have similar VO2max values.

When comparing running economy between or within groups, VO2 should be reported relative to the 0.75 power of body mass rather than simple per-kg scaling, as submaximal VO2 does not increase linearly with body mass.
Increased muscle stiffness that enhances storage and release of elastic energy improves running economy in trained runners.
Reducing braking forces and excessive vertical oscillation through improved mechanics leads to better running economy.
Strength training improves running economy by enabling muscles to utilise more elastic energy and reducing energy wasted in braking forces.
Altitude training improves running economy by enhancing metabolic aspects of skeletal muscle that facilitate more efficient oxygen use.
Reliable measurement of running economy changes requires standardisation of footwear, time of test, and nutritional status to limit typical error of measurement.
